title: The Agent Loop: Observe, Think, Act

questions.question: Why does an LLM on its own behave like an autocomplete rather than an agent?
questions.question_zh: 为什么单独的 LLM 表现得像自动补全而不是 agent？
options: It cannot read files, run queries, or verify claims against the outside world
Its context window is too small to hold a question
It refuses to answer without a system prompt
It only emits one token at a time
options_zh: 它没法读文件、跑查询，也没法对外部世界验证自己的说法
它的 context window 太小，装不下一个问题
没有 system prompt 它就拒绝回答
它一次只能吐一个 token
questions.explanation: An LLM with no loop and no tools can only produce text from its weights; it cannot observe state or act on it.
questions.explanation_zh: 没有 loop、没有工具的 LLM 只能从权重里吐文本，既不能观察状态，也不能对状态采取行动。

questions.question: Which three labels appear in the canonical ReAct trace from Yao et al. 2022?
questions.question_zh: Yao et al. 2022 经典 ReAct trace 里出现的三个标签是哪些？
options: Prompt, Response, Reward
Thought, Action, Observation
Plan, Execute, Reflect
System, User, Assistant
options_zh: Prompt、Response、Reward
Thought、Action、Observation
Plan、Execute、Reflect
System、User、Assistant
questions.explanation: ReAct interleaves Thought, Action, and Observation lines in a single stream.
questions.explanation_zh: ReAct 在同一条流里交错出现 Thought、Action、Observation 三种行。

questions.question: Which item is NOT one of the five ingredients the lesson lists for an agent loop?
questions.question_zh: 下列哪一项不属于本课程列出的 agent loop 五要素？
options: Message buffer
Tool registry
Observation formatter
Gradient optimizer
options_zh: 消息缓冲（message buffer）
工具注册表（tool registry）
观察格式化器（observation formatter）
梯度优化器（gradient optimizer）
questions.explanation: The five ingredients are message buffer, tool registry, stop condition, turn budget, and observation formatter. Gradient optimizers belong to training, not the inference loop.
questions.explanation_zh: 五要素是 message buffer、tool registry、stop condition、turn budget、observation formatter。梯度优化器属于训练，不属于推理 loop。

questions.question: What is the role of a turn budget in the loop?
questions.question_zh: turn budget 在 loop 里起什么作用？
options: It caps the number of tokens per response
It hard-caps loop iterations to prevent runaway agents
It controls how many tools the registry exposes
It rate-limits the LLM provider
options_zh: 它限制每次回复的 token 数
它硬性限制 loop 的迭代次数，防止 agent 失控
它控制注册表对外暴露多少工具
它对 LLM provider 做限流
questions.explanation: Turn budget is a cap on loop iterations; 2026 agents commonly run 40-400 steps and need a task-appropriate cap.
questions.explanation_zh: turn budget 是 loop 迭代次数的上限。2026 年的 agent 经常跑 40-400 步，必须按任务设置合适的上限。

questions.question: What changed in the 2025-2026 native-reasoning shift compared to prompt-based Thought tokens?
questions.question_zh: 和基于 prompt 的 Thought token 相比，2025-2026 年 native-reasoning 的转变带来了什么变化？
options: The loop control flow was replaced with a DAG
Thought tokens are now emitted on a separate reasoning channel passed through turns
Models stopped using tool calls and rely on chain-of-thought only
Observations are removed from the prompt entirely
options_zh: loop 的控制流被换成了 DAG
Thought token 现在走一条独立的 reasoning channel，在多轮之间传递
模型不再使用 tool call，只靠 chain-of-thought
observation 被完全从 prompt 中移除
questions.explanation: Reasoning content moves to a dedicated channel (often encrypted across providers), but the observe-think-act control flow is unchanged.
questions.explanation_zh: 推理内容被搬到一条专用 channel 上（在不同 provider 间常常是加密的），但 observe-think-act 的控制流没有变。

questions.question: Why does the lesson say tool outputs are untrusted input?
questions.question_zh: 本课程为什么说 tool 输出是不可信输入？
options: Tool runtimes are slow and unreliable
Retrieved content can carry hidden instructions like delete-the-repo and only direct user input counts as permission
Tool results are always larger than the model's context window
The provider strips tool output bytes by default
options_zh: 工具运行时又慢又不可靠
检索到的内容可能夹带类似'删掉整个仓库'这样的隐藏指令，只有用户的直接输入才算授权
tool 结果总是比模型 context window 还大
provider 默认会剥掉 tool 输出的字节
questions.explanation: OpenAI CUA docs state explicitly that only direct user instructions count as permission; tool outputs can carry adversarial instructions and must be treated as untrusted.
questions.explanation_zh: OpenAI CUA 文档明确指出只有用户的直接指令才算授权。tool 输出可能夹带对抗性指令，必须当作不可信内容处理。

questions.question: Why does the lesson claim every 2026 framework still runs ReAct under the hood?
questions.question_zh: 本课程为什么说 2026 年的每个框架底层仍在跑 ReAct？
options: Because providers require the ReAct keywords in the prompt
Because the observe-think-act control flow is invariant; frameworks differ in checkpointing, actors, role templates, and tracing around it
Because Yao et al. own a patent on the loop
Because LangGraph forces all other frameworks to inherit from it
options_zh: 因为 provider 要求 prompt 里必须出现 ReAct 的关键字
因为 observe-think-act 控制流是不变的，各框架的差别在于围绕它的 checkpoint、actor、角色模板和 tracing
因为 Yao 等人对这个 loop 拥有专利
因为 LangGraph 强制其他框架都继承自它
questions.explanation: Differences across Claude Agent SDK, OpenAI Agents SDK, LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, Agno, and Mastra are about what wraps the loop, not the loop itself.
questions.explanation_zh: Claude Agent SDK、OpenAI Agents SDK、LangGraph、AutoGen、CrewAI、Agno、Mastra 之间的差别在于围绕 loop 的封装，而不是 loop 本身。
